params.name: Aesop's Fables
params.description:
On Feb 16, 560 BC, Aesop was murdered in Delphi, his fables started to be told by folks.
Containing 357 stories, Aesop's Fables is the world's oldest and most influential collection of fables. Originally a collection of folklore in Aesop's time, it was compiled into a fable collection by a Byzantine monk. The majority of the fables use animals as metaphors to criticize the atrocities of aristocrats while also teaching people valuable life lessons. The fables are concise and easy to understand …
